MOTION TO DISMISS
Off Calendar Feb-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Greg Haynes, Esq Telephone 415-546-0777, appearing for Plaintiff;
Nicholas G. Soter, Esq., Telephone 415-355-1941, appearing for Defendant Belamy Miller;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court modifies its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BELAMY MILLER MOTION TO DISMISS

Defendant Bellamy Miller's motion to dismiss the action for delay in prosecution pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 583.410 and 583.420(a)(2)(B) and California Rules of Court 3.1340 and 3.1342 is granted. The complaint in this action was filed on October 24, 2016. Plaintiff failed to exercise reasonable diligence in the prosecution of this action. He did not serve defendant with the summons and complaint for over six months, causing the Court to issue an order to show cause. The delay was not attributable to settlement negotiations or discussions. Plaintiff was not reasonably diligent in either seeking or responding to discovery. Plaintiff failed timely to respond to proper discovery requests, resulting in an order granting Defendant's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and awarding monetary sanctions against Plaintiff and his counsel. In the more than two years after the complaint was filed, plaintiff did not serve any discovery. (See Landry v. Berryessa Union School Dist. (1995) 39 Cal.App.4th 691, 699 [trial court properly dismissed action, where despite "plaintiffs' claims of diligent discovery efforts, they allowed seven and one-half months to go by without activity on the case" and allowed 11 months to elapse before commencing settlement discussions].) Plaintiff also failed to exercise diligence to schedule or attend Court-ordered arbitration of the matter, causing the Court to issue multiple orders to show cause and the appointed arbitrators to lose jurisdiction. Plaintiff has not carried his burden to excuse his lack of activity or to show excusable delay. (See Lopez v. State of California (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1292, 1295; Oskooi v. Fountain Valley Regional Hospital (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 233, 239-241 [dismissal is mandatory when there is no showing of excusable delay].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

DEFENDANT, BELAMY MILLER'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories (set one) is GRANTED as follows:

1. Within two (2) days from the date of this order Defendant will provide Plaintiff with a copy of its form interrogatories with a check mark by each interrogatory to which Defendant is asking Plaintiff to provide responses.
